skill.name: meta-picos-generator
skill.description: Generates PI(E)COS structure (Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, Study Design) from Meta-analysis or study titles. Use when the user wants to extract these elements from a title.
skill.summary: Extracts Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, and Study Design elements from clinical study and meta-analysis titles.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 42 static analysis findings are false positives across 3 files (497 lines). The 21 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are text misidentification with no actual crypto code present. The 19 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are markdown code block delimiters and inline documentation references. The 3 'System reconnaissance' and 2 'Hardcoded URL' alerts are benign documentation text. This is a legitimate medical research prompt-based skill with no executable malicious code, data exfiltration, or unsafe behaviors.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged text passages containing algorithmic keywords as weak crypto. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill. The SKILL.md contains documentation text (e.g., 'Randomized Controlled Trial', 'deterministic'), the audit JSON contains evaluation content, and validate_skill.py has a benign argparse description. Zero cryptographic code present.

medium_findings.title: Shell Backtick Reference in Documentation (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged markdown code block delimiters and inline code backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution. The SKILL.md uses standard markdown formatting (```bash, ```json, ```text) for example commands and code blocks. These are documentation references, not executable shell injections. No dynamic command execution exists.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Reference (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged benign documentation text as system reconnaissance. SKILL.md:48 describes output discipline ('keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly'). Audit result at meta-picos-generator_audit_result_v2.json:23 and :27 discusses scientific integrity boundaries. None are reconnaissance indicators.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Single hardcoded URL at SKILL.md:7 is a source attribution link pointing to the skill's GitHub repository. This is standard documentation practice for referencing the source project. No network connections are initiated by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Blocks Flagged as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Example bash commands inside markdown code blocks were flagged as shell backtick execution. These are documentation-only examples showing users how to run validation scripts. The examples use fixed, hardcoded arguments and pose no injection risk.

content.user_title: Extract PICOS Elements from Research Study Titles
content.value_statement: Medical researchers spend hours manually identifying Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, and Study Design from study titles during systematic reviews. This skill automates PICOS extraction into structured JSON, saving time and ensuring consistent categorization across large sets of studies.
actual_capabilities: Extracts Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, and Study Design from clinical study and meta-analysis titles
Removes research type qualifiers like systematic review or meta-analysis from titles before PICOS extraction
Defaults study design to Randomized Controlled Trial when not specified in the input title
Outputs structured JSON with standardized PICOS fields for programmatic use
Leaves missing PICOS elements as empty fields to avoid fabricating unsupported data
Handles both individual clinical studies and meta-analysis titles
limitations: Cannot extract PICOS elements from full-text articles or abstracts, only from titles
May miss non-standard or implicit study design types not mentioned in the title
Extraction quality depends on clarity and completeness of the input title
Not suitable for non-clinical research areas that do not use the PICOS framework

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Study Screening
use_cases.description: A medical researcher conducting a systematic review uses this skill to quickly extract PICOS elements from hundreds of study titles, enabling rapid screening and categorization.

use_cases.title: Evidence Synthesis Training
use_cases.description: A graduate student in epidemiology learning evidence-based medicine practices uses this skill to practice identifying PICOS elements in published research studies.

use_cases.title: Clinical Librarian Support
use_cases.description: A clinical librarian supporting multiple evidence synthesis projects uses this skill to produce consistent PICOS extractions across studies from different clinical specialties.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PICOS Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract PICOS from the following study title. First clean the title by removing research type qualifiers. Then identify Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, and Study Design. Output as structured JSON.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user provides a single study title and needs a quick PICOS breakdown

prompt_templates.title: Batch Title Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process each of the following study titles through the PICOS extraction workflow. For each title, output the cleaned title and the extracted PICOS elements. Flag any titles where elements are unclear.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has multiple study titles and needs consistent PICOS extraction across all of them

prompt_templates.title: Custom Output Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract PICOS from the provided study title. Output the results as a markdown table with columns for each PICOS element. Include the confidence level for each extracted field.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs the PICOS results in a specific format for inclusion in a research report

prompt_templates.title: Meta-Analysis Composition Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this meta-analysis title. First clean it, then extract PICOS. For the Study Design field, identify the types of studies included in the meta-analysis if mentioned. Explain which parts of the title informed each PICOS element.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs detailed analysis of a meta-analysis title with explanation of extraction reasoning

output_examples.input: The Impact of Exercise on Cardiovascular Health: A Systematic Review
output_examples.output: Cleaned title: The Impact of Exercise on Cardiovascular Health. Extracted PICOS: Population refers to general adult population, Intervention is exercise, Comparator is sedentary or standard care, Outcomes are cardiovascular health metrics, Study Design defaults to Randomized Controlled Trial.

output_examples.input: Efficacy of Pembrolizumab versus Chemotherapy in Patients with Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
output_examples.output: Cleaned title: Efficacy of Pembrolizumab versus Chemotherapy in Patients with Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. Extracted PICOS: Population is patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer, Intervention is pembrolizumab, Comparator is chemotherapy, Outcomes are efficacy measures, Study Design includes Randomized Controlled Trials as the included study types.

output_examples.input: Association between Mediterranean Diet and Cognitive Decline in Older Adults
output_examples.output: Cleaned title: Association between Mediterranean Diet and Cognitive Decline in Older Adults. Extracted PICOS: Population is older adults, Intervention is Mediterranean diet, Comparator is standard diet, Outcomes are cognitive decline measures, Study Design defaults to Randomized Controlled Trial.

best_practices: Provide the complete study title including all key elements for the most accurate PICOS extraction results
Remove non-PICOS qualifiers like systematic review or meta-analysis from the end of titles before starting extraction
Review all extracted fields for accuracy, especially study design types that may be implicit in the title
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for extracting data from full article text or abstracts since it is designed for titles only
Do not fabricate missing PICOS elements - leave fields empty when information is not present in the title
Do not use this skill for research outside clinical or medical study contexts where PICOS does not apply

faq.question: What is PICOS?
faq.answer: PICOS stands for Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes, and Study Design. It is a framework used in evidence-based medicine to structure clinical research questions and guide systematic reviews.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze full research papers?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed to extract PICOS elements from study titles only. It cannot process full-text articles or abstracts.

faq.question: What happens when the title does not mention a study design?
faq.answer: The skill defaults to Randomized Controlled Trial when the study design type is not specified in the input title.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical research?
faq.answer: This skill is optimized for clinical and medical research titles. Its effectiveness for non-medical research topics may be limited.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The output is structured JSON with fields for Population, Intervention/Exposure, Comparator, Outcomes, Study Design, and Other. Empty fields are included for missing PICOS elements.

faq.question: Does this skill replace a full systematic review?
faq.answer: No, this skill assists with the initial screening and categorization of studies by PICOS criteria. It does not replace a complete systematic review methodology.
